title: Coastal-scrub bluff proof phase
summary: Coastal-scrub has no vertical traversal while forest has root-hollow, caves, and canopy climbing. This wave adds one compact bluff or dune-ridge proof: a short upward route through windbreak scrub that teaches how coastal plants hold ground against wind exposure, using the existing platform and climbable engine.

execution_order.why_now: Coastal-scrub is entirely flat; one vertical proof adds surprise and ecological teaching.
A short wind-exposed bluff teaches shelter ecology through movement.
Traversal geometry needs readability review at 256x160.

guardrails: Must use existing platform and climbable engine — no new traversal systems.
Bluff should be short (3-5 platforms) and fully recoverable.
Teach wind exposure and shelter through spatial placement of entries.
Keep it cozy, not dangerous — no falls or penalties.
Stay within the existing coastal-scrub terrain width.
